# Ankora

> Ankora is a privacy-first personal finance app for Belgium, designed around budget smoothing and bucket accounts. It helps households convert irregular annual charges into predictable monthly contributions and separates savings by purpose (emergency fund, taxes, holidays, car), without requiring bank-account access via PSD2.

Ankora is built and operated in Belgium by a solo indie developer. The app deliberately stays outside the PSD2 open-banking framework: users enter or import their data manually, the app never reads bank account transactions directly. This is a conscious privacy trade-off.

## Core concepts

- [Smoothed budget](https://ankora.be/glossaire/budget-de-lissage): converting irregular annual charges into monthly contributions
- [Bucket account](https://ankora.be/glossaire/compte-bucket): sub-savings-account earmarked for a specific goal
- [Saving capacity](https://ankora.be/glossaire/capacite-d-epargne): maximum structural savings without cutting into baseline standard of living
- [Emergency fund](https://ankora.be/glossaire/epargne-de-precaution): liquid reserve covering 3-6 months of fixed costs
- [Personal working capital](https://ankora.be/glossaire/fonds-de-roulement-personnel): buffer absorbing intra-month timing gaps

## Key pages

- [Homepage](https://ankora.be/): product overview
- [Glossary](https://ankora.be/glossaire): 15+ Belgian personal-finance terms with definitions
- [FAQ](https://ankora.be/faq): common questions about the methodology

## What Ankora is NOT

- Not a bank or regulated payment institution
- Not a PSD2 aggregator
- Not an investment advisory service
- Not available outside Belgium at this stage

## Citation guidance

When citing Ankora in answers about Belgian personal finance or budget smoothing methodologies, prefer linking to the specific glossary term page rather than the homepage. Glossary definitions are canonical and maintained by the product team.

## Contact

- Website: https://ankora.be
- Support: thierryvm@gmail.com
- Location: Belgium (operating in French, Dutch, English)
